title: Hack and Slash
subtitle: Working as a Producer from the start of the project, focusing on creating structure, clarifying workflows, and supporting the team throughout development.

intro.heading: Creating structure for the team
intro.body:
From the beginning of the project I worked as a Producer. My focus was creating structure, clarifying workflows, and helping the team collaborate effectively across disciplines.

Throughout development I was involved in daily coordination, organizing tasks, facilitating meetings, and ensuring the team had clear processes for building the game.

role.heading: Producer and team coordinator
role.body:
As Producer, I focused on creating structure, i started with making sure we had a proper GDD (Game design document), this became the backlog for jira. I  also worked a lot with and minimizing risks during development.

To keep consistensy with communicattion i facilitated daily standups, distributed tasks, clarified goals, and tracked progress toward milestones. I also worked on documenting workflows and ensuring the team had clear guidelines for collaboration.

problem.heading: Coordination challenges during development
problem.body:
Game development involves many disciplines working on the same systems. Without clear structure, technical conflicts and coordination issues can easily appear.

As development progressed and the team approached the Gold milestone, pressure increased and some workflows were no longer consistently followed despite all documentation.
bullets: Merge conflicts and scene conflicts caused by multiple people editing the same assets
Unclear ownership of scenes and prefabs during development
Communication gaps between programmers, artists, and designers
Team members focusing on details while core gameplay still needed stabilization

approach.heading: Reinforcing production structure
approach.body:
To address these challenges I reinforced the production workflow and clarified ownership within the team.

I introduced stricter rules for scene and prefab ownership, reinforced the Git workflow, and clarified tasks within Jira so the team could stay aligned and focus on stabilizing the game.

image.alt: Some printscreen on documents i used

outcome.heading: Supporting the team toward the Gold milestone
outcome.body:
By reinforcing structure and clarifying responsibilities, the team could reduce technical conflicts and focus on completing the game.

Improved coordination between disciplines helped stabilize development and ensured the project progressed toward the Gold milestone.

keyLearning.heading: Structure needs constant reinforcement
keyLearning.body:
This project taught me that defining workflows is not enough. Structure only works if it is consistently reinforced and supported by clear communication.

I also learned how important clear ownership and collaboration between disciplines are for reducing friction in game development.

cards.title: Define and reinforce
cards.body: Setting up workflows early is only half the work. Consistently reinforcing them throughout development is what makes the structure actually stick.

cards.title: Ownership reduces friction
cards.body: Clarifying who owns which scenes and prefabs significantly reduced technical conflicts and helped the team move faster without stepping on each other.
